messages.content:
CREATE TABLE metric (
    id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    advertiserId VARCHAR(255),
    adgroupId VARCHAR(255),
    campaignId VARCHAR(255),
    click INT,
    spend FLOAT,
    impressions INT,
    cpm FLOAT,
    cpc FLOAT,
    ctr FLOAT,
    campaignName VARCHAR(255),
    adgroupName VARCHAR(255),
    adName VARCHAR(255),
    adId VARCHAR(255),
    statTimeDay DATE,
    type ENUM('facebook', 'tiktok'),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
Change it postgres

CREATE TABLE metric (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    advertiserId VARCHAR(255),
    adgroupId VARCHAR(255),
    campaignId VARCHAR(255),
    click INTEGER,
    spend NUMERIC(10, 2),
    impressions INTEGER,
    cpm NUMERIC(10, 2),
    cpc NUMERIC(10, 2),
    ctr NUMERIC(10, 2),
    campaignName VARCHAR(255),
    adgroupName VARCHAR(255),
    adName VARCHAR(255),
    adId VARCHAR(255),
    statTimeDay DATE,
    type VARCHAR(255) CHECK(type IN ('facebook', 'tiktok')),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
How about dimensions? You forgot that

CREATE TABLE dimension (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    adId VARCHAR(255),
    statTimeDay DATE,
    type VARCHAR(255) CHECK(type IN ('facebook', 'tiktok')),
    metric_id INTEGER,
    FOREIGN KEY (metric_id) REFERENCES metric(id),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE sedo_revenue (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    date DATE,
    domain VARCHAR(255),
    c1 VARCHAR(255),
    c2 VARCHAR(255),
    c3 VARCHAR(255),
    uniques INTEGER,
    clicks INTEGER,
    earnings NUMERIC(10, 2),
    sedoAccountId INTEGER,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

const tonicFinalSchema = new mongoose.Schema(
  {
    date: Date,
    domainId: String,
    domainName: String,
    conversions: Number,
    revenue: Number,
    estimate: Number,
    campaignNameRT: String, // subId1
    adGroupIdRT: String, // subId2
    campaignIdRT: String, // subId3
    adGroupNameRT: String, // subId4
    keyword: String,
    network: String,
    site: String,
    adName: String,
    conversionTime: Date, // timestamp
    device: String,
    hashId: String,
  },
  { timestamps: true }
);

CREATE TABLE tonic_final (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    date DATE,
    domainId VARCHAR(255),
    domainName VARCHAR(255),
    conversions INTEGER,
    revenue NUMERIC(10, 2),
    estimate NUMERIC(10, 2),
    campaignNameRT VARCHAR(255),
    adGroupIdRT VARCHAR(255),
    campaignIdRT VARCHAR(255),
    adGroupNameRT VARCHAR(255),
    keyword VARCHAR(255),
    network VARCHAR(255),
    site VARCHAR(255),
    adName VARCHAR(255),
    conversionTime TIMESTAMP,
    device VARCHAR(255),
    hashId VARCHAR(255),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

Adjust the above according to the below prisma models
model metric {
  id                Int                 @id @default(autoincrement())
  advertiserid      String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  adgroupid         String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  campaignid        String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  click             Int?
  spend             Decimal?            @db.Decimal(10, 2)
  impressions       Int?
  cpm               Decimal?            @db.Decimal(10, 2)
  cpc               Decimal?            @db.Decimal(10, 2)
  ctr               Decimal?            @db.Decimal(10, 2)
  campaignname      String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  adgroupname       String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  adname            String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  adid              String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  stattimeday       DateTime?           @db.Date
  type              String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  created_at        DateTime?           @default(now()) @db.Timestamp(6)
  updated_at        DateTime?           @default(now()) @db.Timestamp(6)
  metric_dimensions metric_dimensions[]
}

/// This table contains check constraints and requires additional setup for migrations. Visit https://pris.ly/d/check-constraints for more info.
model metric_dimensions {
  id          Int       @id @default(autoincrement())
  adid        String?   @db.VarChar(255)
  stattimeday DateTime? @db.Date
  type        String?   @db.VarChar(255)
  metric_id   Int?
  created_at  DateTime? @default(now()) @db.Timestamp(6)
  updated_at  DateTime? @default(now()) @db.Timestamp(6)
  metric      metric?   @relation(fields: [metric_id], references: [id], onUpdate: NoAction,onDelete: Cascade)
}

You are not correct. You are using wrong models. Correct with updated models

model metric {
  id                Int                 @id @default(autoincrement())
  advertiserid      String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  adgroupid         String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  campaignid        String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  click             Int?
  spend             Decimal?            @db.Decimal(10, 2)
  impressions       Int?
  cpm               Decimal?            @db.Decimal(10, 2)
  cpc               Decimal?            @db.Decimal(10, 2)
  ctr               Decimal?            @db.Decimal(10, 2)
  campaignname      String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  adgroupname       String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  adname            String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  adid              String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  stattimeday       DateTime?           @db.Date
  type              String?             @db.VarChar(255)
  created_at        DateTime?           @default(now()) @db.Timestamp(6)
  updated_at        DateTime?           @default(now()) @db.Timestamp(6)
  metric_dimensions metric_dimensions[]
}

/// This table contains check constraints and requires additional setup for migrations. Visit https://pris.ly/d/check-constraints for more info.
model metric_dimensions {
  id          Int       @id @default(autoincrement())
  adid        String?   @db.VarChar(255)
  stattimeday DateTime? @db.Date
  type        String?   @db.VarChar(255)
  metric_id   Int?
  created_at  DateTime? @default(now()) @db.Timestamp(6)
  updated_at  DateTime? @default(now()) @db.Timestamp(6)
  metric      metric?   @relation(fields: [metric_id], references: [id], onUpdate: NoAction,onDelete: Cascade)
}
Your answer is incorrect
There is no field dimensions inside metric.
